01_initial_request.input_data: classical thermodynamics criteria and standard black hole spacetime metrics (Schwarzschild, Reissner–Nordström, Kerr, Kerr–Newman)
01_initial_request.user_intent: To revisit thermodynamic stability by implementing intrinsic necessary and sufficient conditions for stable global and local thermodynamic equilibrium, avoiding misleading results from weaker or incomplete conditions
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Determination of global and local thermodynamic stability status, all possible local heat capacities, and critical phase transition curves

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Classical thermodynamics provides generic criteria for equilibrium. [Gap] These criteria have not been fully utilized in black hole physics, leading to potential misleading results. [Decision] To achieve a rigorous stability analysis, chose to use Hessian Eigenvalue Method to define global thermodynamic stability conditions in energy representation, expecting to obtain strict convexity criteria.

parameters.condition: All eigenvalues of Hessian of energy must be strictly positive

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Defined sufficient conditions for global thermodynamic equilibrium based on positive definiteness of the Hessian matrix.

references.relevance: Provides definition of strong intrinsic global condition for thermodynamic stability.
Standard thermodynamics reference for equilibrium criteria.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Eigenvalue method is one way to check stability. [Gap] An alternative algebraic criterion is needed for verification. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive global stability assessment, chose to use Sylvester Criterion to evaluate positive definiteness of quadratic forms, expecting to obtain conditions on principal minors.

parameters.condition: All principal minors of Hessian must be strictly positive

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Established alternative sufficient conditions for global thermodynamic stability using principal minors.

references.relevance: Details Sylvester's criterion for systems with three independent parameters.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Global stability does not imply local stability. [Gap] Local stability conditions based on heat capacities are needed. [Decision] To achieve local thermodynamic stability assessment, chose to use Heat Capacity Calculation method to identify admissible heat capacities, expecting to obtain positivity conditions for local equilibrium.

parameters.definition: Derivative of entropy in space of variables
parameters.condition: Heat capacities must be strictly positive (C > 0)
parameters.formula: Nambu brackets used for general definition

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Defined classical condition for local thermodynamic stability as positivity of specific heat capacities.

references.relevance: Reference for Hessian matrix, specific heats, and Nambu brackets.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Schwarzschild is the simplest black hole solution. [Gap] Its thermodynamic stability needs revisiting under strict criteria. [Decision] To achieve stability determination for Schwarzschild, chose to use Hessian Eigenvalue Method on the mass Hessian, expecting to obtain global stability status.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Hessian has only one element with sign contradicting stability criteria; Schwarzschild is globally thermodynamically unstable.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Global instability is established for Schwarzschild. [Gap] Local stability status remains to be determined. [Decision] To achieve local stability determination for Schwarzschild, chose to use Heat Capacity Calculation to compute C, expecting to obtain local stability status.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Heat capacity is negative, defining local thermodynamic instability; black hole can radiate/evaporate.

references.relevance: Discusses stability in anti-de Sitter space.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Reissner-Nordström (RN) is the charged generalization. [Gap] Global stability needs assessment using eigenvalues. [Decision] To achieve global stability determination for RN, chose to use Hessian Eigenvalue Method on mass Hessian in (s,q) space, expecting to obtain eigenvalue signs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Eigenvalues differ by signs (lambda1 > 0, lambda2 < 0); RN is globally unstable.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Eigenvalue analysis indicates instability. [Gap] Sylvester criterion provides an algebraic check. [Decision] To verify global instability for RN, chose to use Sylvester Criterion on Hessian principal minors, expecting to confirm positive definiteness failure.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Determinant of Hessian is negative in region of existence; global thermodynamic stability criteria cannot be simultaneously satisfied.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] RN is globally unstable. [Gap] Local stability regions need identification via heat capacities. [Decision] To achieve local stability determination for RN, chose to use Heat Capacity Calculation for C_m, C_phi, C_q, expecting to obtain stability regions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: C_m always positive (stable); C_phi always negative (unstable); C_q positive between blue and orange parabolas (locally stable).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Heat capacities indicate phase transitions. [Gap] Critical curves need explicit identification. [Decision] To achieve phase transition mapping for RN, chose to use Critical Curve Identification to find Davies curves, expecting to obtain divergence/sign change loci.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified Davies curves: blue parabola (extremal), orange parabola (phase transition for C_q), line q=0 (phase transition for C_m).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Kerr solution describes rotating black holes. [Gap] Global stability status is unknown under strict criteria. [Decision] To achieve global stability determination for Kerr, chose to use Hessian Eigenvalue Method on mass Hessian in (s,j) space, expecting to obtain eigenvalue signs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Eigenvalues differ by signs; determinant is always negative; Kerr is globally unstable.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Kerr is globally unstable. [Gap] Local stability regions need mapping. [Decision] To achieve local stability determination for Kerr, chose to use Heat Capacity Calculation for C_m, C_omega, C_j, expecting to obtain stability regions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: C_m positive (stable); C_omega negative (unstable); C_j positive between blue and orange lines (locally stable).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Heat capacities signal phase transitions. [Gap] Critical curves for Kerr need identification. [Decision] To achieve phase transition mapping for Kerr, chose to use Critical Curve Identification to find Davies curves, expecting to obtain divergence/sign change loci.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified Davies curves: blue line (extremal), orange line (phase transition for C_j), line j=0 (regular limit).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Kerr-Newman (KN) is the most general standard solution. [Gap] Global stability with three parameters is complex. [Decision] To achieve global stability determination for KN, chose to use Sylvester Criterion on Hessian determinant, expecting to obtain definiteness status.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Determinant is always negative in region of existence; KN is globally unstable.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] KN is globally unstable. [Gap] Local stability regions for multiple parameters need mapping. [Decision] To achieve local stability determination for KN, chose to use Heat Capacity Calculation for various fixed parameter combinations, expecting to obtain stability regions.

parameters.fixed_parameters: mass, angular velocity, charge, angular momentum, electric potential

02_agent_trajectory.observation: C_m positive (stable); C_omega,phi negative (unstable); Others positive between existence surface and Davies surfaces (locally stable).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Local stability regions are bounded by critical surfaces. [Gap] Davies surfaces for KN need identification. [Decision] To achieve phase transition mapping for KN, chose to use Critical Curve Identification to find Davies surfaces, expecting to obtain divergence loci.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified Davies surfaces bounding regions of local thermodynamic stability for different processes.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparison of derived stability conditions with classical thermodynamics criteria (Hessian positive definiteness, Heat Capacity positivity)

global_stability_status.interpretation: All standard black hole solutions (Schwarzschild, RN, Kerr, KN) are globally thermodynamically unstable.

local_stability_status.interpretation: Local stability exists in specific subregions defined by heat capacity positivity (e.g., RN between parabolas).

critical_curves_identified.interpretation: Davies curves and surfaces successfully derived for all solutions indicating phase transitions.

heat_capacities_calculated.interpretation: All possible local heat capacities for each solution were determined.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully determined the global and local thermodynamic stability status, calculated all possible local heat capacities, and identified critical phase transition curves for standard black hole solutions using intrinsic necessary and sufficient conditions.
